description_long: This object is a large, rectangular solar energy installation featuring multiple solar panels arranged on a flat, ribbed platform. The panels are distributed in a grid-like pattern, with several dark, glassy photovoltaic modules mounted on a metallic or composite base. The structure includes raised support elements and what appear to be access or maintenance features along the perimeter. The mounting platform is robust and likely constructed from metal, providing stability and durability for the solar array. The overall design suggests it is intended for installation on a flat surface, such as a rooftop or dedicated ground pad, in an urban environment. The object is engineered for efficient solar energy capture and integration into a larger power infrastructure.
description: A large, rectangular solar energy installation with multiple dark solar panels mounted on a ribbed metallic platform, designed for urban rooftop or ground use.
description_view_0: Front view shows the rectangular platform with solar panels and raised support structures clearly visible on the surface.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the low profile of the installation and the tilt of the solar panels, with support elements visible along the length.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back edge of the platform and the arrangement of panels, with some access or maintenance features apparent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view reveals the symmetry of the panel arrangement and the robust mounting structure beneath the panels.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 10-15 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly defined, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: roof-mounted or ground-mounted on a ribbed metallic platform with aluminum frame supports
attribute_array_configuration: multiple rows of solar panels arranged in a rectangular grid, approximately 8-10 panels in total
